creator: mlogsd@atshell.com
text:
(In reply to Renato Botelho from comment #6)
register for an api key at
https://upgrade.yubico.com/getapikey/

a 'key ID' will be returned, and will need to be saved.

Then add an authorized key file on the system, ie: /etc/auth_yubi

in this file add a single line in the format

username:xxxxxxxxxxxx

where the value for 'x' is the first 12 characters displayed when tapping the yubikey.

then add the config for pam.  In mine I added /etc/pam.d/yubikey with the contents of

auth            required        pam_yubico.so mode=client id=<keyid from registration process> authfile=/etc/auth_yubi
I just include the yubikey pam config in each service I want pam to use it for

cat /etc/pam.d/sudo
#
# $FreeBSD: releng/11.2/etc/pam.d/su 219663 2011-03-15 10:13:35Z des $
#
# PAM configuration for the "su" service
#

# auth
auth            sufficient      pam_rootok.so           no_warn
auth            sufficient      pam_self.so             no_warn
auth           include         yubikey
auth            requisite       pam_group.so            no_warn group=wheel root_only fail_safe ruser
auth            include         system
